title: Robbie Williams admits using AI in writing: ‘It knows me back to front’
author: Holly Patrick

content.full_text: Robbie Williams admits using AI in writing: ‘It knows me back to front’ Robbie Williams says that he has used artificial intelligence to help him with writing on his Instagram page. Robbie Williams says that he has used artificial intelligence to help him with writing on his Instagram page. The former Take That star, 52, spoke to CNBC as he celebrated 30 years as a solo artist.…

ai_output.summary: Robbie Williams has revealed that he uses artificial intelligence (AI) to assist with writing. He stated that the AI "knows me back to front" implying it has a good understanding of his style and work. This admission came during an interview on CNBC to mark 30 years as a solo artist. Williams made this comment about using AI on his Instagram page, but the article does not specify which particular projects or songs were assisted by AI.
ai_output.summary_v2: Robbie Williams admits using AI to assist in songwriting, stating it "knows me back to front", marking the first public confirmation of his collaboration with artificial intelligence in creative work. He made this statement to CNBC while celebrating 30 years as a solo artist. No further details are provided about how he used AI or what specific projects it was involved in.

technical_terms.explanation: Artificial Intelligence is a computer system that can think and learn like a human, often used for tasks such as recognition, decision-making, and problem-solving.
Artificial Intelligence refers to the broader field of study and development involving algorithms, models, and systems that simulate human intelligence.

title: How France’s shift from Windows to Linux reflects broader push for digital sovereignty
author: Tech Desk

content.full_text: How France’s shift from Windows to Linux reflects broader push for digital sovereignty This is the latest step by France to reduce reliance on US tech giants and strengthen its digital sovereignty by adopting locally developed software and cloud services. France seems to be gearing up for a major shift in its public sector tech stack, as the government looks to move several Windows-powered computers to Linux in an effort to cut dependence on US-based …

ai_output.summary: France is transitioning from Windows to Linux in a bid to reduce its reliance on US tech giants and promote digital sovereignty. This move is part of the country's efforts to adopt locally developed software and cloud services. The shift aims to strengthen France's independence in the digital sphere. Several Windows-powered computers are set to be replaced with Linux-based systems. This initiative reflects France's broader push for self-sufficiency in technology.
ai_output.summary_v2: France's government is transitioning from Microsoft Windows to Linux to reduce reliance on US tech giants and promote digital sovereignty. This shift involves moving thousands of computers to locally developed software and cloud services. The goal is to strengthen France's control over its digital infrastructure.

technical_terms.explanation: A free and open-source operating system that can be used on a variety of devices, allowing users to modify and customize the code.
A proprietary operating system developed by Microsoft for personal computers, providing a user interface and platform for running applications.

The tone of the piece is critical and implies that Altman's shortcomings in this area are notable.

title: TCS jobs: Fresher recruitment drops to 25K amid demand uncertainty in IT sector
author: Biplob Ghosal

content.full_text: TCS jobs: Fresher recruitment drops to 25K amid demand uncertainty in IT sector TCS has scaled down fresher hiring for FY27 to 25,000 offers, significantly less than FY26's 44,000. This reduction is driven by demand uncertainty. Mumbai: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), India’s largest IT company, has made job offers to only 25,000 freshers in 2026-27. The IT giant said that the hiring of more college graduates will depend up…

ai_output.summary: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) has scaled back its fresher recruitment for FY27 to 25,000 job offers, down from 44,000 in FY26 due to demand uncertainty in the IT sector. This marks a significant reduction in hiring by TCS, India's largest IT company. The company attributed the decrease to uncertain demand, which may impact future hiring of college graduates. TCS made 25,000 freshers job offers for 2026-27. The exact reason behind the reduced hiring is unclear, but uncertainty over market conditions is cited as a factor.
ai_output.summary_v2: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) has reduced fresher recruitment by nearly 43% for FY27, offering just 25,000 jobs compared to 44,000 in FY26 due to demand uncertainty in the IT sector. This significant drop comes despite being India's largest IT company. The hiring of more college graduates will depend on market conditions and industry demand.

title: The real space science behind 'Project Hail Mary'
author: Tara Haelle

content.full_text: The real space science behind 'Project Hail Mary' The science fiction blockbuster wowed audiences with its depiction of space travel and more. Here's what NASA staff and other scientists say about the basis for the amazing events of the film. This story contains major spoilers. The film Project Hail Mary has just blasted past the milestone of $400 million revenue globally and is generating early Oscar buzz. And the film's depiction of in…

ai_output.summary: NASA staff and scientists have weighed in on the science behind the movie "Project Hail Mary", which has surpassed $400 million in global revenue. The film depicts a solo space mission to save Earth from an alien threat, but experts say that sending a single person into deep space is not currently feasible. However, NASA does have plans for future manned missions to Mars and other destinations. Some events in the film are inspired by real-life space exploration, such as the use of solar sails and the concept of a planetary defense system. The film's depiction of alien life forms has sparked debate among scientists about the likelihood of extraterrestrial life existing elsewhere in the universe.
ai_output.summary_v2: "Project Hail Mary" has surpassed $400 million in global box office revenue, with NASA staff and scientists crediting its accurate portrayal of space travel and astronomy. The movie's success is generating early Oscar buzz, reflecting its well-researched science fiction storyline. The film's depiction of space-related events was praised by experts for its attention to detail.

title: West Asia crisis, geopolitical upheaval to top agenda at 3-day naval commanders' conference

The three-day conclave will also focus on enhancing operational readiness, capability development, and fostering international cooperat… New Delhi: Top commanders of the Indian Navy will deliberate on implications of the West Asia crisis in the context of safeguarding India's energy security during a three-day conclave beginning Tuesd…

ai_output.summary: The Indian Navy's top brass are meeting in New Delhi for a 3-day conference to discuss the West Asia crisis and its impact on the country's energy security. The conference will also focus on enhancing operational readiness and developing capabilities. Top commanders will deliberate on the implications of the crisis and how to safeguard India's energy supplies. The conclave aims to foster international cooperation. The meeting begins today, Tuesday.
ai_output.summary_v2: Top naval commanders from the Indian Navy are meeting in New Delhi for a 3-day conference to address the West Asia crisis and its impact on India's energy security. The conference will focus on enhancing operational readiness, capability development, and international cooperation. This meeting comes as India seeks to safeguard its energy interests amid growing regional instability.

title: Electrification at the core of energy vision, says minister
